zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Beauchamp於大約1244年出生在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘時，她的父親Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch11242歲，母親Alice de Mohun23歲。她於1267年與Hamon de Massey VI在英國爾鄧翰 梅西結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1309年在英國享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Beauchamp于大约1244年出生在英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘时，她的父亲Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch11242岁，母亲Alice de Mohun23岁。她于1267年与Hamon de Massey VI在英国爾鄧翰 梅西结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1309年在英国享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Beauchamp was born about 1244, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, her father, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, was 11242 and her mother, Alice de Mohun, was 23. She married Hamon de Massey VI in 1267, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1309, in England, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Beauchamp est née environ 1244, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch, avait 11242 ans et sa mère, Alice de Mohun, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Hamon de Massey VI en 1267, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1309, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Beauchamp um 1244 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch 11242 und ihre Mutter Alice de Mohun 23. Sie hat 1267 in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England Hamon de Massey VI geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1309 im Alter von 66 Jahren in England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Beauchampが約1244年にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップで生まれた時，父Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatchは11242歳，母Alice de Mohunは23歳であった。1267年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイでHamon de Massey VIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1309年にイギリスで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1244년 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch(은)는 11242세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Mohun(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1267년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 Hamon de Massey VI(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1309년 영국에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Beauchamp родилась около 1244 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch было 11242 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Mohun, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hamon de Massey VI в 1267 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1309 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 66 лет.
